summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Base64 image upload feature. All backend API tests (8/8) passed. Frontend E2E tests verified image upload, storage, and display across admin panel and public blog pages.

issues: Console warning: Duplicate extension names found: ['link', 'underline'] in TipTap editor - minor, does not affect functionality

critical_code_review_comments: Base64 image upload implementation is correct and follows best practices
Image validation (file type, size) is properly implemented on backend
Frontend correctly handles Base64 data URLs in img src attributes
No security issues found - authentication required for upload endpoint
seed_data_creation: Test blog post 'TEST_Base64_Image_Post' created and cleaned up during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Base64 image upload feature fully tested and working. Image upload endpoint at POST /api/blog/upload-image converts images to Base64 data URLs. Blog posts store Base64 images in featured_image field. Both admin panel and public blog correctly display Base64 images.

image_upload_endpoint.details: POST /api/blog/upload-image returns Base64 data URL for PNG, JPEG, GIF, WebP files. Validates file type and size (max 5MB). Requires authentication.

blog_post_with_base64_image.details: Blog posts can be created with Base64 featured_image. Data persists in MongoDB and is retrieved correctly.

admin_dashboard_thumbnails.details: Admin dashboard displays Base64 image thumbnails correctly in post list.

blog_editor_image_upload.details: Blog editor shows image preview when Base64 URL is entered. Upload area and URL input both functional.

public_blog_listing.details: Public blog page displays Base64 images in post cards correctly.

individual_blog_post.details: Individual blog post page displays Base64 featured image correctly.

bug_fix_verification.original_issue: Blog images uploaded via admin panel were not persisting in deployed environment due to ephemeral filesystem
bug_fix_verification.fix_applied: Refactored image upload to use Base64 data URLs stored directly in MongoDB
bug_fix_verification.verification_result: PASS - Base64 images persist correctly and display on all pages
